what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard signal item that records the operator's decision for the global `visual-fidelity` decision, selecting the option 'Push the drawn style further first', and states that work gated on `decision://global/visual-fidelity` may RESUME with that option.
observations: YAML frontmatter carries id, board:// address, type=signal, source=claude_code, state=raised, title, author_session=operator, and a single attached_to link to decision://global/visual-fidelity
created and updated timestamps are identical and include timezone offset
history array records a single state transition from null to raised by operator with note 'filed'
The body restates the chosen option and explicitly frames the resume step as belonging to the lane/operator
channel and thread fields are empty
standouts: The chosen option is 'Push the drawn style further first' for the visual-fidelity decision
State is 'raised', indicating an active signal rather than a draft or resolved notice
It is explicitly attached to decision://global/visual-fidelity
It uses 'decision.decided signal' semantics and tells gated work it can RESUME
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format with YAML frontmatter and a board:// URI address
Uses a history array to track state transitions with actor and timestamp
Uses ISO 8601 timestamps with explicit timezone
Uses a typed link (attached_to) to relate the signal to a global decision resource
concerns: created and updated are identical even though the item is in a 'raised' state that may later change
No rationale for why this option was chosen is captured
channel and thread are empty, so routing/discovery context is minimal
notes: A raised decision signal that commits the visual-fidelity option and tells blocked downstream work it can resume.
questions: What alternative options were considered and rejected?
Which specific lanes or tasks are gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity?
Who or what is responsible for consuming this signal and performing the actual resume?
What does 'Push the drawn style further first' concretely change in implementation?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record a decided option and signal that gated work can resume, which matches exactly what the file does.
intention_signals: It appears designed to broadcast a finalized decision to downstream consumers so that work blocked on the visual-fidelity choice can proceed along the agreed drawn-style-first path.
novelty: It encodes explicit resume-gating semantics in a noticeboard signal, treating the decision record itself as the unblocking event.
contribution: It contributes a durable, timestamped, addressable record of a resolved decision option and a machine-readable permission-to-resume signal for dependent work.
summary_for_embedding: A raised noticeboard signal item recording the operator's selection of 'Push the drawn style further first' for the global visual-fidelity decision and stating that gated work may resume.
intention_for_embedding: To notify downstream lanes that the visual-fidelity decision is decided and they can resume work using the chosen drawn-style-first option.
